Patch Set 2:

This should really be two separate patches. One for the per-file lu_env cache, and a second one for the lru changes.

Patch Set 2:

Tests received by maloo, run on CentOS release 6.5/x86_64: (https://maloo.whamcloud.com/test_sessions/baf9aaea-e6a8-11e3-acfd-52540035b04c).  Ran 19 tests.  No failures.

Patch Set 2:

(3 comments)

In general, I would prefer to see the CLIO code work better with the existing kernel pdflush thread to do this.  I would rather see us move toward working better with the kernel VM than implementing more code to duplicate the functionality it is providing.

Patch Set 2:

(3 comments)

Mainly we deal with LRU pages here. Dirty pages are not a concern here.

commit.subject: LU-5108 osc: Performance tune for LRU
commit.message:
LU-5108 osc: Performance tune for LRU

Early launch page LRU work in osc_io_rw_iter_init();
Change the page LRU shrinking policy by OSC attributes;
Delete the contented lock osc_object::oo_seatbelt
Other tiny changes for LRU management
